fr.elements: Main Building; Landscaped Area; Pedestrian Walkways; Perimeter Fencing; Front Driveway
fr.recommendations: Enhancing emergency operations and response effectiveness might be achieved by improving access and visibility around the property. Creating a secondary access point could facilitate smoother entry and exit for emergency responders. Managing and trimming dense vegetation, especially near the main access routes and pedestrian walkways, could improve visibility, aiding navigability and movement. Ensuring that pedestrian paths are kept clear and adequately maintained may support swift responder movement throughout the property. Clearly marked entrances along the perimeter fencing would assist in orientation and streamline navigation. The front driveway could be reviewed for potential improvements to accommodate maneuverability and staging for emergency vehicles.

wildfire.conditions: Tree Cluster; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Scattered Trees; Vegetated Perimeter; Vegetated Perimeter; Defensible Space
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing separation between the tree cluster and the structure, as well as managing the vegetation directly adjacent to the structure, could improve defensible space and reduce fuel continuity. Maintaining and extending the existing open areas around the structure can reinforce fuel breaks and limit the spread of wildfires. Addressing the vegetated perimeter, particularly by increasing separation of the scattered trees, could reduce potential fuel sources and improve conditions for property safety and preparedness. Regular maintenance of these areas may also assist firefighters in accessing and defending the property during a wildfire event. Surrounding the property with well-maintained, low-combustibility vegetation and promoting ongoing vegetation management practices could contribute to long-term wildfire resilience.
